_note: Living spec-clause index for tools/spec-coverage. Each clause carries a spec pointer (file + section heading) — the sync anchor; tests/test_spec_coverage_registry.py fails if a pointer stops resolving. `must_cover` marks the curated safety subset the CI gate enforces (a test tagged @pytest.mark.spec("<id>") must exist). The broader set was enumerated from the specs (grow-spec-registry workflow) and is the denominator; tag more tests to grow the gated subset. A map, not a quality score.

clauses.title: File paths appear in photos-23 only for items requiring user review or a blocker
photos-23 summarizes automatic/no-op GPS decisions per destination rather than enumerating every file path
photos-24 re-derives every automatic GPS decision deterministically from its own validated inputs, never from the photos-23 summary
A held-back fingerprint-mismatch is confirmed on a later run only when the operator sets accept_fingerprint_change:true; otherwise it stays held back
At a formal decision stage the numbered JSON artifact is always produced, even if the result is complete/no-op
A native-GPS photo yields a time-anchor proposal only if close enough to a GPX point or short segment under configured thresholds
All numbered geotag artifacts are written flat into the .photos-ingest/ control directory
Geotag artifacts must never be written inside 6-photos-by-dest or any scanned media folder
All numbered geotag artifacts are grouped by/cover destinations separately and are inspectable per destination
Each metadata write/rename is individually atomic (temp+atomic-rename or safe-write) so an interruption leaves the pre-write or fully-written file, never a corrupt intermediate
Automated GPS is never rolled back: no pre-state stored, no revert op emitted; it is recomputed from current inputs
A proposal may be auto-resolved without an explicit user_decision only when the governing config flag for its class opts in
A bucket is resolved from its own date's evidence or the destination timezone with no inheritance step; a manual offset applies only to that day, not sibling buckets
Each bucket's proposed offset is chosen in priority order: self-anchored, else timezone-derived, else blank manual-required
If 5-photos-by-date still contains photos, geotag must block before creating any geotag JSON artifact
Symlinks under by-dest (file or nested directory) are barred; geotag never follows a link and relies on prep's symlink guard for nested links
A video-class file under by-dest hard-stops geotag (videos belong in 4-videos-by-date), never silently skipped
by-dest must contain only image/raw photo files; any other-class non-media file hard-stops before any JSON, reporting offending paths
Geotag verifies schema/cache versions via the handoff's recorded fingerprints, not by opening and inspecting prep's database directly
Rename planning detects case-insensitive conflicts and rejects unsafe rename plans
Changing a manual GPS value overwrites to the new value while the pinned original stays unchanged for later full withdrawal
A candidate anchors only if its GPX timestamp is within the plausible-clock-error window of the frame's naive time read as UTC, applied during the spatial search
Once time decisions are complete, resolved UTC is computed for every media file and persisted into SQLite
Per-file work may run concurrently under -j but concurrency must never change semantic results
A config or camera-group-classification change restales photos-21 and forces recompute/revalidate of time requirements
Conflicting anchor candidates (outside the chosen cluster) trigger user review
The largest agreeing offset cluster wins (within spread), with point-before-segment then closest as the within-cluster tiebreak
A folder holding only sub-destinations is materialized as a file_less container destination for downward decision propagation
Container destination cells never block and never demand input; each auto-resolves by inheritance yet stays overridable
A container destination carries no clock-offset cells (offsets neither cascade nor roll up)
Artifacts are generated with all human-decision fields already present and empty unless previously filled
Every durable artifact validates upstream, records dependencies, and rejects downstream use if dependencies changed
A destination is the folder directly containing media; nested media-holding folders are their own separate destinations
Results are merged in deterministic path-sorted order so journal, totals, resume facts, and mismatches are identical regardless of job count
The development-subfolder presence check is re-evaluated at every invocation (plan, execute, finalize) and hard-stops before any mutation or bundling
The development-subfolder check is strict: mere presence triggers the hard-stop even if the folder is empty; it never judges whether it really holds output
A ledger entry for a file that no longer exists is kept (not pruned), triggers no operation, and is carried into the archived DB
Authored drift confirmations are preserved across reruns; a confirmation whose bucket no longer triggers is stale-flagged
corrected_offset_seconds is sanity-validated (numeric, within +/-86400); out-of-range leaves the artifact unchanged
While photos-22 requires_user_input the workflow stops and does not create photos-23 or photos-24
The GPS-drift validation gate runs between resolved UTC and GPS planning, consuming complete photos-21 and current-offset resolved UTC
The validated drift offset lives in photos-22 and does not mutate the photos-21 decision it refines
Once photos-22 is complete, resolved UTC is recomputed with each confirmed validated offset, then the SQLite cache and fingerprint are persisted
A bucket is a drift review item iff its offset source is manual/timezone (not GPX self-anchor), it has no native-GPS anchor, and GPX coverage exists
An untouched drift bucket blocks the gate; a zero scrub is never implied by inaction (confirmed:false => requires_user_input)
The plan records photos-22 as a direct dependency so execute rejects a plan whose drift confirmation was withdrawn after planning
Execution must not invent new rename decisions or choose a different suffix; it only applies renames present in photos-24
Execution must not recalculate decisions; it only validates and applies decisions recorded in the executable plan
Each rename is re-verified no-clobber at execute time; an unexpectedly occupied target is a blocker, never a clobber
Execution is allowed only after photos-24 exists, is current, and validates all upstream dependencies
Folder fallback proposal is inherited from the nearest ancestor destination as a confirmable proposal, else manual-required
A file placed from the folder fallback is a manual placement (marker manual_fallback), reversible via the pre-state ledger
Folder fallback is optional and never blocks (requires_user_input always false); un-located photos fall through to remaining Section 23 options
Folder fallback inheritance is recursive parent-first, parent to child only; an authored fallback re-roots the chain
Folder GPS fallback ranks after preserve-native/manual-lock/GPX and before accept-unlocated/block in the GPS resolution order
A geotag JSON existing on disk does not by itself mean execution is allowed
Changing the filename format invalidates the executable rename plan
Filename timestamp format is config-driven via filename_timestamp_format and must not be hard-coded
Filename-format config participates in dependency fingerprints and the extension follows the project's normalization rules
The filename timestamp is computed from resolved UTC converted to destination civil timezone, not raw camera time and not UTC text
Finalize generates photos-26-complete-log.json carrying prep's log forward as a superset, not re-deriving or discarding prep history
Finalize captures the photos-26-geotag-ingest.db backup as a consistent read-only copy of the live DB without mutating it
Finalize is a separate explicitly-invoked command, not part of execute and not run automatically
Finalize writes a self-describing manifest with the SHA-256 of each bundled item, including DB snapshots
Geotag and finalize never perform the merge; merge requires a successfully finalized workspace
Finalize is non-destructive: it reads and bundles, never mutating the workspace, artifacts, live SQLite DB, or library
Finalize requires geotag to have ended successfully (complete executed plan + summary) and refuses an incomplete or stale state
photos-21-time-decisions.json is the first durable geotag artifact; no earlier geotag JSON is produced
Each downstream artifact must include its full flattened dependency set, not only the immediately previous artifact
GPS planning must not begin until every file has valid resolved UTC
GPX folder parsing/fingerprinting (disk-heavy) runs only after in-memory camera-group recognition passes
GPX folder scanning/parsing/fingerprinting must happen before the time-decision stage
The current GPX fingerprint is recorded as a dependency unconditionally whenever a GPX set is loaded (conservative over-inclusion)
If GPX is unavailable or disabled, later artifacts record that no GPX evidence was used
The prep handoff is verified by recomputed content_fingerprint, not its whole-file byte hash, so a run-only re-prep does not restale geotag
If camera-group classification is incomplete, geotag does not create photos-21, prints snippets, and stops requiring config update + rerun
A missing managed 0-6 folder hard-stops geotag (restore + re-run prep); geotag never creates or repairs the structure
After preflight, in-memory file objects are created for files under 6-photos-by-dest only
An invalid user value is preserved and flagged for correction, never silently deleted, coerced, or repaired
Execution journals every metadata write, marker write, move, or rename and updates SQLite/cache after successful writes
A JSON-artifact dependency must name the file and store the SHA-256 of its exact file bytes
The pre-state ledger is per-workspace; a fresh workspace starts with no ledger and pins whatever GPS files now carry as its original
The workspace lock is acquired at startup before preflight; if another run holds it geotag exits fail-fast without scanning, planning, or writing
A manual GPS override is reversible via the pinned pre-state; withdrawing undoes its effect and changing re-applies cleanly
A misplaced root entry (loose file incl. dotfiles, non-managed folder, or symlink) hard-stops geotag strictly
Geotag never plans or applies time/GPS metadata or renames for a video and never reasons about videos in its machinery
The tool never mutates autonomously; the executor acts only on decisions the user wrote into decision fields
Planning never assumes a currently occupied filename will free up via a planned rename; existing disk files are treated as permanently occupied during suffix allocation
The operation set is fixed single-threaded before concurrent work; execution never decides anything concurrently
If any destination_distribution_subfolder (jpg/tif) exists under by-dest, geotag hard-stops before any JSON artifact
JSON artifacts must not be produced for preliminary reports, blocked preconditions, or config snippets
The user fills existing fields or changes boolean/string values only; never adds structure, brackets, keys, or sections
Staleness must not rely only on mtime, size, cached metadata, recursive dependency blocks, or previous validation results
User decision fields must not be silently overwritten
Partial or failed worker output is never journaled as a confirmed operation or cached as a valid fingerprint
The workflow must not preserve outdated automated GPS decisions merely to avoid touching media files
A destination/proposal with no proposal at all keeps requires_user_input true (conservative default)
An artifact must not depend only on the previous artifact and assume its recursive dependency block suffices
Execution must not proceed from stale dependency state
No artifact may be created from stale upstream inputs; no downstream artifact used if a recorded dependency changed
Resolved UTC must not be finalized for files in a destination whose timezone/time dependencies are incomplete
The workflow defines only states/artifacts/dependencies/decision order, not the exact CLI, classes, SQLite/JSON schema, GPX parsing, or metadata write commands
Each (group, destination) offset cell is inferred only from that group's native-GPS frames in that destination
Clock offsets are never inherited from an ancestor destination (unlike timezone and folder GPS fallback)
There is no upward roll-up: a parent's offset is never computed by aggregating its children's cells
An offset cell splits into per-naive-date buckets when a (group, destination) spans more than one day; a midnight crossing splits in two
Camera clock offset is inferred/applied per (camera group, destination[, day]), never once per camera globally
Workflow order is strictly time first, GPS second, rename/metadata execution last
photos-24 must include a flattened dependency list directly naming and fingerprinting all required upstream artifacts and inputs (handoff by content_fingerprint)
An executable plan is produced only when photos-21/22/23 are complete, UTC is current, renames are safe, no blockers remain, and all dependencies are current
The executable plan includes a no-clobber rename only where the corrected local-time filename differs from the current name
After each metadata write the decoded-content fingerprint is verified unchanged; a mismatch holds the operation back, never silently accepted
A prep run must occur after the most recent by-date to by-dest move before geotag runs (hard contract requirement)
On regeneration, existing user-filled decision fields are preserved where the logical target is unchanged
apply_manual only targets files with no native GPS (preserve-native wins), so the captured pre-state is in practice always the absent sentinel
For a manual GPS override the pinned pre-state is committed to the ledger before the GPS write that overwrites it
On first application a manual GPS write captures and pins the file's prior GPS (or an absent sentinel) in the ledger, keyed by content fingerprint
The pinned pre-state is the true original, written once and never overwritten by subsequent runs of the same or changed decision
Multiple time-anchor candidates for a (group, destination) must be ranked, never averaged
The workflow recalculates only the affected downstream artifacts when upstream inputs change; a no-op run is a no-op
Machine-generated proposal/status fields may be recalculated on regeneration
Time/filename recomputation rewrites timestamp and filename in place within by-dest and never moves the file to another destination or location
Before an artifact is used, each named JSON dependency must be re-read and re-hashed and must match the recorded hash
Execution re-hashes JSON deps, validates non-JSON deps, and rejects stale plans before any mutation
Re-running a fully-applied plan is a no-op; execution never reapplies a present write/rename and never chooses a different suffix
Geotag is re-runnable after a successful execute (not a terminal run-once phase), until the workspace is merged/sealed
Residual content in 1-strays/2-missing-metadata/3-redundant-jpgs/4-videos-by-date is tolerated and does not block geotag
Resolved UTC uses the file's (group, destination, date) effective offset plus the destination effective timezone; a file is solvable only when both are complete
On resume execution re-derives each file's actual state: target present skip, source present + matching fingerprint re-apply idempotently, differing fingerprint blocks
Execution is safely re-runnable after a crash/partial application, re-validating all dependencies on re-run
Every human-authored value (config it reads + decision fields) is sanity-validated before use, in addition to hash checks
Geotag operates only on files under 6-photos-by-dest and never geotags files still in 0-5 folders
On a sealed/terminal merge marker geotag hard-stops immediately, mutating nothing and touching nothing, directing to a fresh workspace
On a sealed workspace with files at root/0-sources, geotag warns of a likely new dump and leaves the dump exactly where it is
The journal and SQLite (incl. pre-state captures) go through a single controlled writer; the pre-state capture pass completes before the concurrent write pass
On resume, already-applied or already-satisfied target states are detected and skipped; only remaining operations are applied
The pre-mutation snapshot is labelled for the geotag phase so it never name-collides with a prep snapshot for the same plan id
With snapshots_required, execution aborts before any mutation if a required pre-mutation snapshot fails (snapshots are otherwise optional/disabled by default)
A non-empty 0-sources means an un-processed dump waiting; geotag hard-stops directing the user to re-run prep
A user decision that can no longer be safely applied is preserved and marked stale/requiring-review, never deleted
If prep/by-dest validation fails, geotag prints a stale-state report and produces no JSON artifact
If any dependency does not match, the artifact is stale and must not be used
No-clobber suffix is deterministic: no suffix if base free, first collision -001, second -002, etc.
photos-25 records artifact/run identity, the plan SHA + flattened upstream chain it summarizes, per-dest+global counts, resume facts, failures, status, mismatches, and separate run metadata
photos-25 is terminal and is never re-hashed as an upstream dependency
Final status is success/partial/failed/rejected; a non-empty fingerprint-mismatch list forces partial, and a stale-plan rejection writes no summary at all
Unrecorded by-dest media or missing by-date photos hard-stop geotag with a targeted re-run-prep blocker, not a generic stale message
The plan distinguishes three GPS operation origins (apply manual, revert manual, recompute automated) and the transformation log records which applied
Destination timezone auto-resolves by default (nested geography) without per-destination confirmation, staying overridable
Timezone-derived (and manual) offset proposals are confirmable-only and never auto-applied; only a GPX self-anchor auto-resolves under policy flags
photos-21 must settle the civil timezone for every destination under by-dest, with pre-created decision fields
Timezone inheritance is recursive parent-first, flowing parent to child only, never sibling to sibling
Timezone proposal priority is self-proposed, then nearest-ancestor inherited, then config default, else blank
A manual timezone set at a folder re-roots the inheritance chain there for its descendants
A destination with no self/ancestor/config-default timezone keeps an empty manual field and is marked requires_user_input
An exiftool failure is recorded and leaves the file at pre-operation state; it is never recorded as applied or treated as a no-op next run
Carried-forward fingerprints are verified transitively at the owning step, not redundantly recomputed, but a change still surfaces downstream
A no-anchor bucket with a resolved timezone gets offset = -(civil tz UTC offset at the bucket's earliest naive instant), computed DST-aware
On an uninitialized workspace (no root sentinel) geotag hard-stops with run-prep-first and creates nothing
Unknown camera groups produce directly pasteable config snippets as textual output (not JSON artifacts)
The workflow produces user-facing outputs at each blocked/decision point so the user always knows stage, why blocked, which artifact, and next action
The resolved-UTC cache is created only after validating all upstream dependencies including photos-21
The resolved-UTC cache exposes a deterministic SHA-256 fingerprint over per-file rows plus input fingerprints, stable across unchanged runs
A config edit outside the time-policy subset must not restale already-resolved UTC; the whole-config hash is integrity not the staleness trigger
Sanity-validation runs whenever a value is consumed on a run, never cached as permanently valid
A sanity-validation failure is a hard blocker located precisely; geotag produces no downstream artifact and mutates nothing
The clock-error window is timezone-independent; the destination timezone must not be used to bound matching
Withdrawing a manual GPS decision emits an explicit revert operation driving the field to the pinned pre-state (restore coords or clear)
Execution writes photos-25-execution-summary.json and produces a final execution summary
move between dests re-evaluates under the new dest tz; old values not carried
geotag never records a move: reprep gate blocks, handoff/by-dest untouched
withdraw reverts GPS only; no time/name revert, never relocates across dests
-j1 vs -jN produce identical summary + file tree (geotag and merge)
journal writes originate only on the main thread (geotag and merge)
symlink under 6-photos-by-dest blocks the run and is never followed
Suffix allocation treats existing library names and all incoming files targeting the same destination as occupied
Re-running over a batch already in the library changes the library not at all and reports already_present
Any misplaced workspace-root entry blocks: loose file (dotfiles included), non-managed folder, or symlink
A file that cannot be merged is left in by-dest and the run ends partial without sealing
A blocker is left in by-dest, not lost; merge never deletes an un-merged file
Library-file fingerprints are computed on the fly and cached in the workspace SQLite, fingerprinted at most once per run/across runs while unchanged
A collision is detected per target by a single existence check at placement time (no library-wide index)
A collision is never resolved by overwriting or by assuming equality without a fingerprint
On a name collision the incoming by-dest file is renamed, never the resident library file
Results aggregate in deterministic order so two safe job counts produce the same summary content (modulo timestamps and jobs value)
The library lock is held throughout the concurrent pass; concurrency never relaxes cross-run library exclusion
merge plan/dry-run/execute leave photos-00-config.json byte-identical
bad merge policy -> blocker, no plan, no summary, no placement
Merge does create any missing destination subdirectory under library_root when placing a file
A re-run reproduces the same targets and anti-collision names; it does not allocate a new suffix to an already-placed file
Different-content collision renames the incoming file (library file authoritative and untouched)
If no saved plan is present, dry-run stops and directs the operator to run plan first
Dry-run validates the real saved plan and reports a concise summary, never a separate simulation and never a full dump
Execute confirms the workspace lock and library-side lock are held
On success execute captures photos-35-merge-ingest.db, a consistent atomic copy of the live ingest DB
Execute journals each move's confirmed completion incrementally via a single writer on the main thread
Execute re-confirms each target free immediately before placing, never places onto an occupied target, and never renames/overwrites a library file
Execute writes no SQLite rows; the only SQLite write is the fingerprint cache populated during planning
execute applies the saved photos-30 disposition verbatim, never re-derives
TOCTOU clobber: target occupied by different content at execute -> blocker, source kept, library byte unchanged
A planned-free target occupied at execute by identical content is treated as already-in-library: remove source, no library write
Execute rejects the plan before any mutation if stale, missing, unverifiable, or produced by a different tool/version/schema
Execution revalidates the plan before any mutation and rejects stale plans
The plan's safe-alternative name is used only where the plan already recorded it for an anticipated collision
A merge that fails to acquire either lock, or finds the marker absent, exits cleanly having mutated nothing
A finalized entry whose file is absent from by-dest is not a blocker (already-merged/resume case)
A free target places directly with no library read; fingerprinting happens only on a collision
Merge holds both the workspace lock and the library-side lock for the duration of its run
init-library is the only path that creates the .photos-library marker, writing it no-clobber; a second run is a no-op success
init-library inside a workspace with no path reads library_root from config and blesses it (no config write)
init-library inside a workspace with an explicit path blesses the dir and writes only library_root into config
init-library outside any workspace with no path is an error (nothing to bless, no config)
init-library outside any workspace with an explicit path blesses the dir only and advises re-running from a workspace
Merge inspects the library only at the destination paths its incoming files map to, and only when a path is occupied
An interrupted (not fully successful) merge is not sealed and resumes normally
after a partial run the journal holds only confirmed moves
A library file is never deleted, written, or renamed; read only to fingerprint on a collision
A library file is never deleted or overwritten at any step of the move
The library lock is a .photos-merge.lock dotfile keyed to library_root serializing merges even from different workspaces; cannot-take exits without touching the library
Acquisition order is workspace lock, then library_root validation + marker check, then library lock — both held before any library content access
photos-35-merge-log.json is written by copying photos-26 forward and appending a per-file merge step, deterministically
Merge does not edit photos-26-complete-log.json (phase-artifact ownership)
Merge maps each by-dest destination to a library subpath preserving the curated structure (default identity mapping)
The destination-to-library mapping is deterministic: the same by-dest file maps to the same target every run
marker validated before the library lock: marker-less lib drops no .photos-merge.lock
The move is realized as copy-to-temp, verify, atomic-rename-into-target, then remove source last, preserving atomicity and never losing a file
Merge moves each successfully-placed photo into the library and removes it from by-dest
snapshot + revalidation precede the parallel move pass
Merge never auto-creates the .photos-library marker on the data path
Merge never creates or repairs the workspace 0-6 structure
Merge never deletes or mutates a library file and no file is ever lost
A workspace never merged has no photos-35 log; photos-26 remains its authoritative record (absence reads as 'not merged')
Merge never overwrites the photographic content of a library file
Merge never renames an existing library file
The workspace never keeps a duplicate of a file already in the library
Nothing about the library's state is persisted library-side except the lock
Merge does not perform library-wide deduplication
Merge does not build or maintain a full index of the library
Merge never mutates by-dest content or alters a file's name except a library-collision incoming rename
stale-plan rejection writes no photos-31 summary at all
merge placement leaves EXIF time/GPS byte-identical (both placement primitives)
Merge does not tear down the workspace or clear leftovers (a separate operator decision)
Merge does not follow a symlink into or out of the managed tree
Merge does not perform the jpg/tif development breakout (a later library-side phase)
Merge does not write into, overwrite, or invalidate another phase's artifacts; a never-merged workspace retains the full geotag-time record intact
A no-op re-prep refreshing only run metadata does not trip the post-finalize currency check
Merge must not alter the library's existing organization; only additive placement
Merge must not re-organize, re-deduplicate, or re-fingerprint by-dest content (prep's fingerprints are reused)
All library placements are no-clobber, planned and re-verified at execute time, performed atomically or atomic-equivalent
The plan's by-dest enumeration is built from the finalized record (handoff joined to photos-24 renames by fingerprint), not a disk-name scan
Planning mutates nothing; the only durable side effects are writing photos-30-merge-plan.json and populating the fingerprint cache
Per-file preconditions record the by-dest content fingerprint (reused from prep) plus the file's current size/mtime stat-ed at plan time
plan mutates nothing: by-dest + library byte-identical, no 31/35/journal/seal
Merge recomputes the current handoff content_fingerprint and hard-stops 're-run geotag and re-finalize' if it differs from the one photos-24 recorded
preflight blocker -> no summary, library byte-unchanged
by-dest photo absent from finalized set -> re-run-prep blocker, no placement
Merge does not recompute the final name; the only name change it introduces is the anti-collision rename of the incoming file alone
Merge reads an existing library file only to fingerprint on collision, otherwise leaves it untouched
After a failed (all-blocked) or rejected (pre-mutation abort) summary, re-planning is allowed (nothing moved)
Merge requires 0-sources empty, confirming no un-processed dump in the inbox
library_root must carry the .photos-library marker; a missing marker hard-blocks directing 'run init-library first'
Merge re-verifies by-dest is the clean photo-only set (no photos in 5-by-date, 6 only image/raw, no jpg/tif dev subfolder), else blocks
Merge requires the workspace finalized (archival package + photos-26 log exist), else hard-stops 'run finalize first'
Merge requires an executed photos-24 plan with photos-25 summary status success, else blocks (never merges a half-geotagged set)
Merge hard-stops with 'run prep first' if the workspace is not initialized (root sentinel absent)
A missing managed 0-6 folder hard-stops merge directing the operator to restore it
On success merge automatically re-seals the archival package to include the merge log, summary, and DB snapshot, rewriting the manifest SHA-256s
Re-seal re-bundles current artifacts by reading them; it does not re-run geotag's finalize logic or re-derive geotag's record
4-videos-by-date, 2-missing-metadata, 3-redundant-jpgs, 1-strays residuals are allowed and do not block merge
The renamed-in/source-unlink-pending crash window is safely recognized and completed; no file lost or half-present under its final name
Resume re-derives each file's state from the filesystem plus its recorded content fingerprint (not-yet/placed-not-removed/fully-moved)
Resume is via execute; plan refuses while a prior merge is in flight but not sealed (partial or success summary + saved plan, unsealed), directing execute
Same-content collision is recorded already_present: place/rename nothing, remove the by-dest source
The durable seal marker, not by-dest emptiness, is the authority for 'this workspace is finished'
Seal and re-seal happen only on full success; a partial/failed/crashed run writes no seal and leaves the workspace re-runnable
The terminal seal marker is recorded as a separate photos-00-sealed.json alongside the guard, not a field in the guard
After sealing, every media-mutating script (prep/geotag/merge) refuses on this workspace; prune-quarantine is the sole exception
If the workspace carries the terminal/sealed marker, merge hard-stops immediately touching nothing
merge sealed path warns but relocates nothing, library untouched
required pre-mutation snapshot fails -> abort before any placement, no seal
The by-dest source removal is always the last step for a file, after its library copy is verified; a crash before leaves it safely in by-dest
A by-dest source is removed only after its verified copy is in the library; un-merged files stay in by-dest
The by-dest source is removed only after the library copy is verified in place, so a crash never loses a file
A fully successful merge leaves by-dest empty of photos
Anti-collision suffix is append-at-max+1 (not gap-filling), never reusing a previously-allocated number on a re-run
photos-31-merge-summary.json is written to .photos-ingest/ on finishing; never an upstream dependency, never re-hashed
Counts partition the batch into placed-new, renamed, already-present, blocked (each merged file in exactly one of the first three); removed = batch − blocked
Final status is one of success, partial, failed, or rejected (pre-mutation abort records snapshot/abort detail for audit)
The summary is grouped by destination with a global summary and separates run-metadata timestamps from fingerprints
The summary records per file: by-dest path, final library path, renamed-for-library, already-present, removed-from-by-dest
The summary records the SHA-256 of photos-24/photos-25/handoff that pinned the merged state
Merge sweeps stale .tmp-xdev-*.part temps from targeted directories at the start of execute and records the count
The step-1 temporary uses the recognizable deterministic name .tmp-xdev-*.part
Textual output separates moved/already-in-library/left-in-by-dest and confirms no library file was renamed/overwritten and artifacts written
If a library file's fingerprint cannot be computed, the item is a blocker: no overwrite, no blind rename-duplicate, no guessed identity
If the archival package was already copied to permanent storage, photos-35 and photos-31 are added so it stays current
library_root must resolve to a valid existing directory outside the workspace/managed tree; empty/malformed/nonexistent/workspace-internal hard-blocks
The copy is verified by size and content fingerprint against the known by-dest fingerprint so a torn copy is detected
Merge writes only its own 3X artifacts and copies photos-26 forward rather than editing it
Allowed operation types are limited to mkdir, move_no_clobber, rename_no_clobber, quarantine_move, db_upsert, db_remove
On ambiguous match or unpreserved mtime prep does not guess: it scans the by-dest file as new and ghost-prunes the old row
Every durable artifact carries a depends_on block recording the upstream fingerprints it relied on
A video under 5/6 or an image/raw under 4-videos-by-date is a prep hard block: report the path, produce no plan
The post-init root-entry block message states dumps belong in 0-sources and points there
If any recorded dependency is stale, missing, or unverifiable, prep blocks before creating or using the downstream artifact
Blockers are reported textually; the workflow never silently skips them
Prep must never move/rename/quarantine/delete/touch/metadata-write any file inside 6-photos-by-dest
A by-dest copy is never quarantined; it can only ever be the retained file
By-dest facts that influenced any decision are recorded so execution rejects the plan if by-dest state changed affecting uniqueness
Byte-level SHA-256 is reserved for artifacts (JSON + config); media is never identified by a byte hash
The cache distinguishes move, content-change, different-file, dup-already-in-by-dest, and dup-already-quarantined
Prep caches the enumerated per-file fields: camera identity, uncorrected timestamps, native GPS, normalized media facts, and a raw payload
prep applies its plan sequentially: cache writes only on the main thread
Cache updates are derived from the validated plan and applied during execution, never speculatively during planning
For unmutated files with stale cache (changed size/mtime or stale metadata) a db_upsert records the refreshed state
The controlled cache update commits only after the corresponding filesystem effect is verified, converging cache to filesystem truth
Candidate lists are sorted before submission and worker results aggregated deterministically
Each file is classified by canonical lowercase dotless extension into exactly one of image/raw/video/other
Destination-name collisions are resolved in memory at planning via the -NNN allocator, treating every on-disk and planned name as occupied
Concurrency must never change semantic results
Every human-authored config value prep consumes is sanity-validated before use (separate from fingerprint staleness)
A malformed gpx_root path (illegal chars, not a path) is a validation blocker
Numeric/enumerated config and the folders/media_extensions lists are validated for type, range, uniqueness, and no class/dir collisions
Workspace and control paths must resolve to sane syntactically valid paths; required paths must be non-empty
filename_timestamp_format must produce a non-empty filesystem-safe component with no separators or illegal chars
An invalid zfs dataset/pool or snapshot-name prefix (whitespace, /, second @) is rejected before planning
A config validation failure is a hard blocker that names the field, produces no plan and no mutation, and never silently coerces or repairs
Carried-forward identity never overrides a content change; a differing size/mtime forces a rescan
A crash mid-init leaves no sentinel; the next run re-enters init harmlessly, leaving already-created folders and the dump in place
Prep execution is safe to re-run after a crash: it finishes outstanding work without redoing completed work or double-applying
Date-organized names follow YYYY-MM-DD/YYYY-MM-DD--HH-MM-SS[-NNN].ext; missing-metadata uses UNKN_<base>[-NNN].ext (flat)
Timestamped media is grouped into a YYYY-MM-DD/ day subfolder under its band while the filename keeps the full timestamp
The end-of-prep DB backup snapshot photos-15-prep-ingest.db (consistent atomic copy) is captured on overall success
Planning derives operations in a fixed deterministic stage order, updating an in-memory current path per file
dry-run is read-only: control dir + quarantine byte-identical on disk
If no saved plan is present, dry-run stops and directs the operator to run plan first
dry-run is not a simulation: the summary derives from the real serialized plan execute will consume, never a separate VFS walk
dry-run reads the saved plan, validates it, and reports a concise summary plus the plan path, not every operation
A mutable-side file matching a different by-dest file is a duplicate: by-dest is retained and only the mutable-side copy is quarantined
An empty 0-sources is normal steady end-state and is not a missing-folder condition
At the end of prep the workspace holds a complete self-sufficient audit record (prep log, DB backup, live DB, handoff/summary) standing on its own
Each quarantined file's manifest entry is written before the file is moved into quarantine (evidence-before-quarantine)
Execution must not patch or recompute a stale plan, and SQLite writes must never come from uncontrolled worker threads
execute reads the saved plan from the canonical path; if absent it stops and directs the operator to run plan first
Execution never re-derives organization decisions; it applies only the operations recorded in the plan after validation
execute revalidates every dependency and per-file precondition and rejects the plan before any mutation if stale/missing/unverifiable or from a different tool/version/schema
execute takes a pre-mutation snapshot where configured (zfs block), honoring snapshots_required
Immediately before each move/rename/quarantine, execute re-confirms the target does not exist (case-insensitive) and acts atomically, treating an unexpected target as a blocker not a clobber
Long-running execution is visible via phase-level log lines and live aggregate progress (TTY in-place / plain when redirected)
Media-extension lists are config and participate in the field-scoped fingerprint, restaling organization stages on change
Files already inside a managed folder are left where they are at this stage, except a by-date file not in its conforming day folder
On reconciliation the filesystem is authoritative; the cache is reconciled to it via move-aware/ghost-prune, re-fingerprinting where identity is unprovable
The fingerprint algorithm including tool identity/version is recorded in the cache and handoff
If prep moves/case-normalizes without content change, carry the known fingerprint forward to the new workspace-relative path
If a fingerprinted media file is absent from cache, compute and store its decoded-content fingerprint
Media content fingerprint is over decoded content (ImageMagick identify for photos, ffmpeg stream MD5 for videos), not a byte hash
A content fingerprint failure for a non-by-dest media file whose equality a decision depends on is a hard block
A fingerprint failure on a media file never implies equality and blocks any content-equality decision
The identify pixel fingerprint is invariant under in-place metadata writes and renames, giving stable identity
If size/mtime changed treat the cached fingerprint as stale and recompute (a metadata-only edit recomputes to the same value)
Reuse the cached fingerprint when path exists with unchanged size/mtime and fresh metadata context
A forbidden sidecar (.xmp/.dop/.pp3) is a hard block
Genuinely missing cache rows are pruned with db_remove (ghost prune)
If gpx_root resolves inside the managed 0-6 tree, prep must skip that subtree so GPX files are never organized
Prep does not read, parse, or fingerprint GPX files
Remaining files (mutable and by-dest, excluding paired JPEGs and other-class) are grouped by (content_fingerprint, media_class)
A top-level content_fingerprint = SHA-256 over the canonical serialization with run-scoped fields (incl. nested per-run audit) removed pins the deterministic content
content_fingerprint is computed with itself excluded so the value can live inside the file without being self-referential
The handoff is deterministic for a given workspace state (sorted lists, no unordered-iteration reliance), with run metadata/diagnostics segregated
The handoff includes the enumerated minimum: versions/tool, run_metadata, fingerprints, folders+mutability, sorted file list, camera/by-dest facts, metadata status, depends_on
The handoff is machine-readable and non-editable, written only after execution/journal/filesystem/cache all match, and never edited in place
The handoff manifest is written only on overall success; a crash before it leaves no handoff so geotag won't act on an incomplete run
Run metadata (plan_id, execution_id, diagnostics, journal pointer) is segregated under run_metadata, not at the top level
1-strays is excluded from the handoff's folders-scanned because strays are not deterministic downstream content
When the only change is a user move, the handoff is updated to the new locations via carried-forward identity without re-reading moved files
A dedicated sweep moves dotfiles arriving in a dump area (root on init, 0-sources any run) to quarantine and prunes emptied hidden-dir skeletons
On an uninitialized workspace prep creates any missing part of the 0-6 structure and the control directory
Init's base move excludes the managed 0-6 folders and control dirs, never moving a structure folder into 0-sources
Init moves every non-managed base entry (loose files and whole folder trees, structure preserved, no flattening) into 0-sources
Hidden dot-prefixed dump entries are swept to recoverable quarantine, leaving no hidden litter at the root
Job count is run metadata, not a semantic dependency, unless it genuinely changes planned behaviour
All media mutations are journaled
The prep log is carried forward (not discarded); geotag's finalize consumes it as the prep portion of the complete log
The prep log is derived from the validated plan and journals, introducing no new authority
Prep must not emit a log that looks complete but reflects only the last run; it fails or writes a clearly-partial log if retained history is insufficient
The prep log is maintained incrementally: a move-only re-prep updates affected entries from carried-forward identity; a no-op run leaves it unchanged
The prep log is per-photo, content-fingerprint-keyed, human-readable JSON, pretty-printed and stably ordered

The prep log records each file's ordered prep journey (ext normalization, initial consolidation only on first run, redundant-JPEG sep, dedup/quarantine, organization, provisional rename), attributed per run
The prep log records quarantined duplicates (origin, quarantine path, retained counterpart, fingerprint) so the set-aside is auditable
The prep log is complete and self-sufficient: a full honest account of prep transformations even if geotag/merge never run
A cached metadata record is reusable only when all freshness inputs match (path/move-aware, size, mtime, fingerprint, extractor/field-set/schema/group-key versions)
A missing managed 0-6 folder on an initialized workspace hard-stops mutating nothing, names the absent folders, and does not auto-recreate
2-missing-metadata stays flat with no date grouping
A file left in 2-missing-metadata is residual and never blocks geotag
no special re-import path from 2-missing-metadata
On a unique move match prep carries cache identity forward, drops the stale old row, and records the new by-dest row
Before treating a disappeared cached file as a ghost, prep applies move-recognition; a move explained by a by-dest entry is not a ghost
Move recognition only updates prep's cache/handoff and performs no filesystem operation on read-only by-dest
Each planned move/rename carries enough facts to update the cache row without re-fingerprinting after the move
When new files appear, the new plan acts only on files requiring action; existing files are scanned but not moved/re-fingerprinted unnecessarily
All media operations are no-clobber: planned no-clobber and re-verified no-clobber at execute time, performed atomically
Prep must not rename/relocate caused by corrected timestamps
Prep does not flatten 0-sources; dumped folder trees are left on disk and files are not lifted to the top level
Prep must not expose any geotag command
Prep must not expose geotag JSON generation or validation
Prep must not plan or execute GPS metadata writes
Prep must not write GPSProcessingMethod markers
Prep must not do GPX matching/interpolation/extrapolation
journal is evidence not a resume script: corrupt/deleted journal still re-plans from filesystem
Prep must not do manual GPS fallback planning
Prep never parses meaning from a non-conforming filename; it re-ingests it as an ordinary dump and takes the timestamp from metadata
Prep does not perform the user's by-date->by-dest move and does not enforce geotag preconditions
Prep does not reconcile the old quarantine manifest entry with a restored file; the old entry remains a historical record
Prep does not re-normalize already-normalized extensions
Prep does not re-quarantine already-quarantined duplicates
No downstream artifact is created from stale upstream inputs
Dotfiles inside band folders, 1-strays, or read-only by-dest are not in a dump area and are left untouched
Prep must not plan, infer, write, or apply any time or GPS correction
Prep must not do time-offset calculation
Prep must not make camera-time policy or folder-timezone decisions
Prep uses the raw camera-naive timestamp only for organization; it does not correct it, resolve timezone, or convert to UTC
Prep must not plan or execute EXIF/QuickTime timestamp writes
A no-op re-run refreshes only run_metadata leaving content_fingerprint unchanged (no needless restale); a real content change does restale
A non-lowercase extension is renamed to lowercase via two no-clobber renames (unique temp, then final), collisions suffix-allocated
Organized outputs always land at a unique destination path (uniqueness per day folder), never physically flattening 0-sources
other-class files get no content fingerprint by design; its absence is normal and never a blocker
other-class files are never fingerprinted, deduplicated, date-organized, or blocking; carried as not_applicable and moved to strays
Paired JPEGs are excluded from content-dedup so a RAW+JPEG pair is never treated as a duplicate of itself
Partial or failed tool output is never persisted as a valid cache record; it surfaces as a clear blocker, never a silent success
Passive extraction/caching of geotag-needed fields is allowed and required, but making decisions from them is not
The handoff/summary reports passive grouping facts per camera group and per by-dest folder, with no inference
Prep caches metadata strictly passively: stores facts, makes no time/GPS decision, and corrects nothing
A planned destination colliding with an existing by-dest path (incl. case-insensitively) must not clobber; allocate a safe name or block, by-dest untouched
The persistent ImageMagick driver must reset per-image state between commands and delimit each file's signature output unambiguously
External tools run as persistent workers where supported (exiftool -stay_open, ImageMagick command-stream); ffmpeg is spawned per file
The plan is written to the canonical photos-10-prep-plan.json (no path flag); dry-run/execute read it there and plan prints the location
plan() applies no operations: 0-sources byte-identical, no journal/handoff
plan produces a serialized plan with id, versions, command, config fingerprint, ordered ops, blockers, warnings, preconditions, dependencies, and summary
After init the workspace base contains only the managed folders and control/dot dirs, never loose files or stray folders
The end-of-prep audit log photos-15-prep-log.json is written only on overall success
Progress output is never the only record of a mutation; the journal is durable, progress is transient and never a dependency
Empty day folders left behind are pruned
prune-quarantine preserves the managed 0-6 tree
prune-quarantine is exempt from the seal guard and still runs on a sealed workspace
The quarantine directory is excluded from scanning so re-runs neither re-organize nor re-quarantine already-quarantined files
Every other mutable-side copy in a duplicate group is moved to quarantine via quarantine_move
Every run's summary reports the quarantine footprint (total files, size, distinct plan_id dirs, oldest/newest plan id)
ordinary prep plan never auto-deletes quarantine
Each quarantined file gets a manifest entry recording original path, quarantine path, retained counterpart, and duplicate evidence
file restored from quarantine re-flows as a normal dump (no un-quarantine path)
If config is present prep reads it as-is and treats the workspace copy as authoritative
Prep recognizes a by-date->by-dest move cache-only (size/mtime/basename) and must not re-fingerprint or re-extract the moved file
Prep recognizes a re-sort between destinations within by-dest the same cache-only way, performing no filesystem operation
Prep recognizes files already in their correct location and does not move them
Recorded dependency fingerprints include at least the enumerated set (config, CLI, per-file preconditions, schema/algo/extractor versions, by-dest preconditions, final fingerprint)
A by-date file not in its conforming day folder is re-located into the correct day folder (idempotent no-op if already there)
Re-planning never clobbers a prior plan: the existing artifact is renamed aside under the -NNN suffix and its backup announced
Prep re-plans from current state and never patches/resumes an interrupted plan; the stale plan is superseded
Prep reports no_op / already_correct facts rather than treating prior work as error
Re-prep is mandatory whenever new media enters by-dest, including after a prior successful geotag
init never moves a dump folder named like a managed folder beneath itself
A recorded destination change updates the handoff and restales the affected destination decisions so geotag re-evaluates the moved file
A restored file still duplicating a retained copy is re-quarantined under the current plan_id; otherwise organized normally
In a duplicate group one file is retained by folder priority (by-dest > 5 > 4 > 3 > 2 > 0-sources)
Prep reuses cached fingerprints and metadata for unchanged files
plain root dotfile on an initialized workspace hard-blocks
Any misplaced root entry on an initialized workspace (loose file, non-managed folder, or symlink) is a hard block
Prep does not treat a stray root folder as an additional inbox to sweep; a folder dump belongs inside 0-sources
A non-dot symlink at the root is barred (escape), checked before the file/folder distinction, even on an uninitialized workspace
Files route by class and timestamp: video+ts->4, photo+ts->5, media-no-ts->2-missing-metadata, non-media->1-strays
The whole run (plan/dry-run/execute) runs under one workspace-wide lock acquired at startup and held until exit
Prep produces the same destination names for the same unchanged input state
The media scanner skips .git, .photos-ingest/, .photos-ingest-quarantine/, dotfiles, and 1-strays as whole subtrees
Stage 0 scans 0-sources and every managed folder, applies the guards, and splits inventory into mutable-side vs read-only by-dest files
The seal check runs at startup right after the lock, preceding any scan or plan
On a sealed workspace with a new dump (root or 0-sources), prep reports the likely new dump and that it must be moved to a fresh workspace, leaving it untouched
A sealed/terminal workspace hard-stops media-mutating commands immediately, mutating nothing, directing the user to a fresh workspace
A second run on an unchanged workspace produces zero media mutations
Config is seeded at run start before any config-dependent fingerprint, so fingerprints derive from the on-disk file not the in-code defaults
If photos-00-config.json is absent prep seeds it from the in-code default template
The workspace-guard sentinel is written last, only after the full 0-6 structure and control dir exist and the run succeeds
Within a (folder, basename) group containing a RAW and a .jpg, the JPEG is redundant and (if in 0-sources) moved to 3-redundant-jpgs
Where a pre-mutation ZFS snapshot was taken, the operator may roll the workspace back as a clean-slate recovery path
Prep is the sole writer of the cache/identity content via the controlled single-writer path; phase DB regions are disjoint
Prep is the sole writer of the config file, only seeds it once, and never edits it thereafter (user hand-edits)
After Stage 5, 0-sources is left empty (every file organized, set aside, quarantined, or moved to strays)
During plan, prep probes each distinct stray extension via exiftool MIMEType and warns if image/* or video/*, suggesting a config add
Stray-media detection is advisory: it never blocks, never reclassifies on its own, and never mutates config
1-strays is excluded from the processing scan; prep writes to it but never scans it, so parked strays are never re-swept
Strays are inert: not fingerprinted, not in the cache, not in any phase log, not in the archival package
Each run uses its own <plan-id> strays subfolder so successive runs never collide and nothing is overwritten
Non-media files are moved into 1-strays/<plan-id>/ preserving their relative path under 0-sources
First file at a name takes the bare name; -NNN suffix added only on collision, lowest-free, no-clobber against a case-insensitive occupied set (not monotonic)
The summary confirms durable artifacts were written only after dependency validation
The summary confirms by-dest was scanned read-only and not mutated
Prep produces a textual summary clearly separating the enumerated categories of operations and facts
Any symlink among managed files (file symlink, nested directory symlink, managed-folder-as-symlink, root dump symlink) is a hard blocker, never followed or silently skipped
The organizing timestamp is read passively from cache in priority DateTimeOriginal -> CreateDate -> ModifyDate
Prep tolerates already-populated by-date and by-dest folders
Filesystem traversal and stat collection are single-threaded and deterministic, fixing the candidate list before concurrent work
Two safe job counts produce the same semantic plan and the same dependency fingerprints for the same workspace
Unchanged already-fresh files produce no operation and are reported as no_op / already_correct
A media file with an unknown fingerprint is not classified as a duplicate; mutable-side is a blocker, by-dest is skipped
Files with unknown/invalid fingerprints are not grouped as duplicates
Videos must never appear in 5-photos-by-date or 6-photos-by-dest (videos live only in 4-videos-by-date)
Prep inventories, deduplicates, and date-organizes videos into 4-videos-by-date, named by date
A worker crash is recovered by safe restart and a transient per-file failure is retried a bounded number of times before the file becomes a blocker
Each phase brings the record up to date additively and owned; it never rewrites or invalidates an earlier phase's file (copies forward and appends)
Each numbered artifact is owned by the single phase whose number it carries; only that phase ever writes it
At the end of each phase the workspace holds a complete, self-sufficient, human-readable audit record of everything done so far that stands alone if no later phase runs
The archival package is produced by an explicit finalize/archive command, not automatically at the end of each geotag
Finalize runs under the workspace lock and is non-destructive — it reads and bundles, mutating neither workspace nor library
A phase must never overwrite/truncate/mutate/extend another phase's artifact; it reads earlier phases' artifacts read-only and copies forward to its own phase-named file
No later phase is a prerequisite for an earlier phase's record being whole; an absent later phase reads as 'not advanced past phase N', never 'incomplete record'
The package contains config, all current decision/plan artifacts (same bytes validated), the live DB + per-phase DB snapshots, and the per-phase transformation logs present
Re-running a phase may refresh its own artifacts but still never touches another phase's
The package is self-describing: records workspace identity, plan/execution ids, and a manifest of its contents with their SHA-256s

Cross-filesystem placement preserves atomicity-equivalent guarantees: copy to temp on destination → verify → atomic rename into verified-free target → for a move, remove source last, so a crash never loses or half-presents a file
Cross-fs temps use a recognizable deterministic .tmp-xdev-*.part name; merge sweeps stale temps from targeted directories at start of execute and records the count (hygiene, inert either way)
An in-place metadata write is atomic (temp + atomic rename, or safe-write mode) so a crash leaves the intact original or the fully-written file, never a corrupt intermediate
A move/rename/placement is a single atomic filesystem operation so an interruption leaves either pre-state or post-state, never a partial result
Human-decision fields are authored by the user and never silently overwritten; required fields are pre-created so the user fills values not structure
Every executed operation is traceable to its recorded decision and fingerprinted inputs via decision artifact → fingerprints → plan → journal

Every mutation traces to an explicit recorded human decision or a deterministic rule on recorded inputs — never an autonomous/unexplained tool choice (machine proposes, user disposes, executor acts only on the disposed plan)

Withdrawing a manual GPS override undoes its effect via a pinned pre-state ledger (restores original coords or clears); automated GPS recomputed; time/filename recomputed in place, never relocating the file
Geotag reuses prep's camera_group_key and never recomputes identity
A group is 'known' only when its key is listed under phones or fixed_clock_cameras
Prep computes camera_group_key from device-identity metadata and emits it in both the handoff and SQLite
camera_group_key derivation is versioned by CAMERA_GROUP_KEY_VERSION in fingerprints so a derivation change is detectable, not silently mixing old/new keys
CAMERA_GROUP_KEY_VERSION is a code constant, not a config field; user never sets the key-derivation version
A config edit outside the time policy re-plans but does NOT restale already-resolved UTC
media_extensions lists are config; the image/raw/video class vocabulary (other=residual) is code; an extension maps to exactly one class
In-code CONFIG is only a seeding template; once the file exists the workspace copy is authoritative regardless of later code defaults
Managed folder names are config but the roles and 0-6 structure invariant are not; logic references folders by role not literal name
Folder-set and media-extension fingerprint changes restale the geotag executable plan and merge plan
Config is SHA-256'd and folded into the dependency cascade like other artifacts
Config lives in .photos-ingest/ which prep skips, so it is never treated as media or folded into the media cache fingerprint
init-library may write only the library_root key into config; no other key
geotag plan + execute leave the workspace config byte-identical
Prep is the sole writer of config in the data pipeline; the user hand-edits other changes
Prep seeds photos-00-config.json from default_config() if absent (external defaults file wins over built-in); if present prep uses it as-is
All three phases read one shared workspace config; phases never maintain separate configs
Staleness is field-scoped/surgical; the whole-file hash is NOT used as the surgical staleness trigger (no collapsing caches onto it)
Every user-supplied config value is subject to the Section 14 input-validation discipline before use
Whole-file config hash is carried in plan depends_on as a coarse catch-all trigger so any config edit restales the plan
All control/artifact files live inside .photos-ingest/; nothing pipeline-related sits among media or at the workspace root
Hidden dotfiles arriving in a dump area (root on init, or 0-sources) are swept to recoverable quarantine, not merely skipped
Prep skips .photos-ingest/ as a whole subtree so a control file can never be mistaken for a photo
Prep skips .photos-ingest/, .photos-ingest-quarantine/, .git as whole subtrees, plus dot-prefixed files and 1-strays/ entirely
The three live-DB writer regions are disjoint (prep cache/identity, geotag GPS pre-state ledger, merge library-fingerprint cache); no two phases write the same rows
The SQLite DB is a durable archived artifact, not throwaway scratch; it is bundled in the package
Merge never indexes or scans the library; it fingerprints a library file only on a collision and caches the result in the workspace DB
There is exactly one database (the workspace photos-00-ingest.db); the permanent library has no database of its own
A DB snapshot is written to a temp name then atomically renamed (clobber-on-refresh of the same-phase snapshot); an interrupted capture leaves the prior or the complete new file, never corrupt
A DB snapshot must be a transactionally consistent image (e.g. online-backup/VACUUM INTO), not a raw byte-copy of a possibly-open file
Each phase captures one retained point-in-time DB backup snapshot at its successful end; re-running a phase refreshes only that phase's snapshot, leaving other phases' untouched
A phase that does not complete successfully writes no new snapshot, leaving the prior one intact
Validate upstream → create artifact → record dependencies → reject downstream use if dependencies changed
A media fingerprint is decoded-content identity that stays the same across EXIF/GPS rewrite and rename
The handoff keeps a whole-file SHA-256 for integrity but uses a content-scoped fingerprint for staleness so a no-op re-prep does not restale downstream
An artifact hash is a byte-level SHA-256 that changes on any byte change (the dependency check for artifacts/config)
dry-run reports a concise summary computed from the real serialized on-disk plan (not a virtual-filesystem simulation), not a full dump
A failed operation leaves the file at its pre-operation state, records the failure, and never records the op as applied so resume does not treat it as done
The execute-time no-clobber check applies to every content-bearing operation: prep moves/renames/quarantine, geotag renames, and merge library placement

No-clobber is re-checked at execute time, not assumed from the plan: immediately before each mutation verify the target does not already exist (case-insensitively); an unexpected existing target is a blocker, never a clobber
Prep/geotag pick the first-free name (bare-first); merge appends at max+1 (append-only, reproducible across re-runs)
Geotag recomputes the timestamp from corrected destination-local civil time and rewrites the name to its final value
A non-conforming existing filename is treated as an ordinary dump, never specially migrated/parsed; the timestamp comes from metadata not the name
Prep's timestamp is from the raw camera-naive source, provisional — not corrected/timezone-resolved/UTC-converted
Both phases name files from one shared config-driven filename_timestamp_format key (never hard-coded), which participates in the dependency cascade
A colliding timestamp gets a zero-padded -NNN suffix allocated no-clobber against a per-run case-insensitive occupied-name set (every on-disk and planned name treated as occupied)
An uncorrected file's provisional and final names coincide, so geotag plans no needless rename
Geotag owns all GPX behaviour; the GPX fingerprint participates in geotag's dependency cascade as upstream of the affected artifacts
If gpx_root resolves inside the managed tree, prep must skip that subtree during scanning so GPX is never organized even under misconfiguration
GPX is consumed only by geotag; prep never reads/parses/fingerprints/organizes/moves GPX files
gpx_root must resolve outside the managed 0-6 media tree
A run-only refresh of the handoff does not restale downstream (content fingerprint unchanged)
Handoff is hashed despite living in the skipped dir; geotag verifies it by recomputing content_fingerprint, not exact bytes
A file is moved/renamed/metadata-written only on a real difference; an already-correct file produces a no-op, not a re-apply
A run changes only what needs changing; a run over unchanged inputs is a no-op (zero mutations, stable equal result, reports cache hits not error)
Only artifacts whose dependency fingerprints depend on a changed input are regenerated; unaffected artifacts are left as-is
Work already done and still valid is reused, not recomputed; expensive ops run only for genuinely new/changed inputs
Library identity is a single empty .photos-library marker created once by init-library; merge performs no other structural inspection and the data path only reads it, hard-stopping if absent
The library is never rescanned wholesale and never mutated except by additive no-clobber placement; no library-wide index, de-duplication, or renaming
library_root is validated like every configured path and must be outside the workspace and its managed 0-6 tree
Lock acquired at startup before any planning/scanning/fingerprinting/metadata-extraction/dry-run/execution
the whole-run lock covers execute, not just plan
Every command that reads or touches the workspace acquires the single workspace lock
lock-contended plan produces no plan artifact and no journal
At most one pipeline invocation may run against a workspace at a time (total mutual exclusion; no concurrent prep/geotag/merge/finalize/prune combination)
Lock is released on both normal exit and error
A stale lock from a crashed process is detectable (owner identity/liveness/start time) and recoverable
Stale-lock takeover is conservative: only a lock whose owner is provably gone may be reclaimed
The transformation log is one file per phase, each produced by copying the predecessor forward under its own phase name and extending the copy; each is a strict superset
Each transformation that followed an authored decision references that decision so the why is attached to the what
A photo's log entry accumulates steps as it advances; each phase only appends its own steps and never rewrites earlier ones
A phase fails or warns/produces a clearly-partial record if retained history is insufficient; it must not silently emit a log that looks complete but only reflects the last run
The log is pretty-printed, stably/deterministically ordered, human-legible JSON that is itself the readable artifact
The log is keyed by content fingerprint (decoded content, surviving rename/move/EXIF write); names cannot key it; other-class files are not fingerprinted or logged as journeys
The log records manual-GPS apply/change/withdraw and the pinned pre-state captured or restored, so a reversal is as traceable as the application
Records the log is built from are retained across runs in enough detail to reconstruct each file's per-file history; per-run journals are not discarded or truncated to a single run
Starting jpg/tif development inside the workspace before geotag is settled is a hard-stop
Finalize is required before merge
Geotag proceeds only when: 5-photos-by-date has no photos, by-dest is photo-only, no dev subfolder exists, and by-dest is prep-consistent (re-prepped)
Geotag rerun is safe: preserved human decisions, already-placed GPS recognized as no-op, and already-finalized renames recompute to the same name
Geotag is freely re-runnable any number of times (including after a prior successful execution) up until the workspace is merged
New photos never reach geotag behind prep's back — files prep has not recognized are not in the hashed handoff
Re-prep is mandatory after the latest by-date→by-dest move (stat-only, no re-fingerprint/re-read), carrying cache identity forward
A rerun does exactly as much work as changed inputs require; an upstream change may pull affected artifacts back into the decision loop while leaving unaffected ones untouched
The user moves PHOTOS ONLY into 6-photos-by-dest; by-dest stays photo-only (no non-media, no videos)
A file that cannot be merged (blocker) is left in by-dest and keeps the run partial WITHOUT sealing, so the operator can resolve and re-run
On a collision with differing fingerprints, merge gives the incoming file a safe differentiating name and places it, leaving the library file untouched
On a collision with equal fingerprints (same content), merge writes/renames nothing in the library, removes the by-dest source, and records the file already_present
A library file's fingerprint is computed on the fly at collision time and cached so the same library file is never fingerprinted twice across runs; the by-dest fingerprint is reused from prep's cache
Merge is idempotent/resumable: a re-run recognizes files already in the library, completes any interrupted source removal, applies only outstanding moves, and a fully-merged re-run is a no-op
Merge takes a library-side lock keyed to library_root in addition to the workspace lock, so no two merges write the same library concurrently even from different workspaces
The library lock is fail-fast and stale-detectable like the workspace lock; a merge that cannot take it exits without touching the library
Library files are never renamed and never overwritten by merge; on a collision it is the incoming by-dest file that is renamed, never the library file

Merge confirms the .photos-library marker before acquiring the lock (order: workspace lock → library_root validation + marker check → library lock), so no .photos-merge.lock is ever dropped into a non-library directory
Per-file move is copy-to-temp → verify fingerprint → atomic rename into the verified-free target → only THEN remove the by-dest source as the last journaled step, so a crash never loses a file
Merge moves (not copies) each placed photo, removing it from by-dest; a fully successful merge leaves by-dest empty of photos; already-present files are also removed from by-dest
Merge moves finalized photos into the library removing merged sources; incoming files may be renamed but library files are NEVER renamed, overwritten, or deleted
Merge builds no library-wide scan/index and does no library-wide de-duplication; it touches only destination paths and inspects an existing library file only on a path collision
Merge requires geotag ended successfully and the workspace was finalized (archival package + photos-26 log exist)

Merge writes photos-31-merge-summary.json recording per-file and total final library paths, renames, already-present, removed, and blockers; its summary/log are terminal records, never re-hashed as dependencies
Merge re-seals by bundling now-current artifacts as a read; it does not re-run geotag's finalize logic or re-derive geotag's portion of the record
On successful merge the archival package is re-sealed automatically (re-bundled with merge log/summary/DB snapshot and refreshed manifest), not by a separate command
Merge re-validates the finalized plan is current with present by-dest (recomputed handoff content fingerprint) and hard-stops if a by-dest photo postdates finalize
Each phase's plan/decision artifact lives at a fixed canonical control-dir path; plan writes it there, dry-run/execute read it from there
A validate/apply command that finds the canonical plan missing stops and directs the operator to plan first
No command takes a path flag for where to put or find its plan; the canonical location is the contract
A planning command prints the path it saved to
Re-planning never clobbers a prior plan/decision file: when content differs the existing artifact is renamed aside under -NNN and the backup announced
A byte-identical re-run writes nothing and backs up nothing, so the decision loop accumulates no redundant backups
An empty by-dest is NOT a reliable clean-slate signal; the durable terminal/sealed marker, not folder state, is the authority for whether a workspace is finished
The seal is a separate photos-00-sealed.json alongside the guard (recording merged/run-id/library_root), not a field in the prep-owned guard
sealed-workspace new-dump path warns but leaves the file exactly in place
A partial merge (blockers) or a failed/crashed run writes no seal marker, so the workspace stays open for re-run
prune-quarantine is the sole op permitted on a sealed workspace

Every media-mutating script (prep, geotag, merge) checks the seal marker at startup (after lock+guard, before scan/plan) and on its presence hard-stops mutating/touching nothing — no confirming re-run, sweep, or recovery utility
photos-00-sealed.json is a separate file written alongside the guard (not a field inside the guard); its presence seals the workspace
Merge reaches success only when every by-dest photo is moved/confirmed and no un-merged photo remains; on success the workspace is marked terminal/sealed
Snapshots are disabled by default unless explicitly configured via the zfs block; ZFS is not assumed/required
Snapshots are keyed by plan id so each plan's pre-mutation state is independently identifiable
When snapshots_required is false/unconfigured, execution proceeds without a snapshot
Pre-mutation snapshots are optional; the pipeline runs fully and safely without them
When snapshots_required is true, failure to take a required snapshot is fatal and execution aborts before mutating
A taken snapshot occurs after dependency-validation and lock acquisition but before the first mutation
On an initialized workspace, any root entry other than managed folders + control/dot dirs is a hard block (loose file, non-managed folder, or symlink); 0-sources is the only inbox
Structural guards are evaluated at each phase's startup after the lock and seal check, before any scan/plan/mutation
The managed 0-6 structure is never silently rebuilt; a missing managed folder on an initialized workspace is a hard block naming it, and prep does not auto-recreate on a non-init run
Symlinks are barred everywhere among managed files (file, nested dir, managed-folder-as-symlink, root dump): never followed/organized, a hard blocker reporting the path with no plan/artifact produced
Base-is-folders invariant does not apply to an uninitialized workspace (first dump triggers init), except a root symlink is still barred
Manual GPS coords must be in-range (lat -90..90, lon -180..180) and well-formed; manual UTC offsets must parse within sane bounds
Enumerated/structured decision values must be the right type/permitted value and match the artifact's shape; a structurally broken decision is a validation error, not silently ignored
Every human-authored value (config + decision fields) must be sanity-validated before use, in addition to the dependency cascade, each run at consumption
Validation fails closed before mutation: a failure is a hard blocker that produces no downstream artifact and mutates nothing
filename_timestamp_format must produce a non-empty filesystem-safe component with no path separators or illegal filename chars
A validation error names the offending file and field; validation never silently repairs a value
Threshold/numeric config (GPX distances/times, job counts) must be the right type and within sane ranges (e.g. non-negative)
Config paths must resolve to a sane valid path of the expected kind, contain no illegal chars, not escape required bounds, and not be empty/whitespace-only
An invalid authored value is preserved and flagged for correction, not deleted/erased
Any user IANA timezone must be a real resolvable zone, not an arbitrary string
The ZFS snapshot name prefix/dataset names must be valid snapshot components; an invalid prefix is rejected before any snapshot is attempted
